quote_of_day.text: > >be me, go on larp about hating subhumans online > got triggered irl by actual human beings who dont give a fuck about ur edgy takes > >tells u to fuck off in 3 words or less > ah yes, the internet’s master race collapses facefirst into reality henlo anon u hit peak cringe today
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of celebrity speculation moved into the board this morning, bringing with it a scattered drizzle of gossip about an Indian idol group featuring 'Gobarjeeta Kumari'.

The discussion was brief but persistent, with Anon weighing in on the potential lineup and musical style.

A front of religious debate blew in during the afternoon, with one anon characterising Hinduism as a philosophy and way of life, but refusing to elaborate.

This system stalled over the board, producing little in the way of clear skies or resolution.

A cold snap of criticism hit the board in the evening, with taxes on foreign goods and reading habits coming under fire.

A high-pressure ridge of discontent also developed, with anon criticising the physical appearance of North Indian women and another claiming to have reevaluated their hate after meeting people from 'hated' groups.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on Indian Idol Group Featuring 'Gobarjeeta Kumari'
articles.body:
Ambedkar kicked off the discussion, inquiring about a potential Indian idol group called 'gobarjeeta kumari girl group', questioning if anyone's sister was auditioning.

The original poster expressed curiosity about the group's prospects, comparing them to DEL48 and speculating on the success of Korean versus Japanese international branches.

Respondents like Mococo and Fuwawa chimed in, discussing the perceived appeal of idols and the seeming advantages of secretly dating one.

Mococo referenced a controversy involving a VTuber named Rushia, while Fuwawa noted that idols have to appear pure and single to appeal to their audience.

The conversation took a turn as users like Haggu, Ozymandias, and Lungi shared their thoughts on the potential group's dynamics and the hypothetical appearance of its members.

Lungi described a stereotypical 'poopjeeta' with 'sunken dark eyes' and 'dark knees', while Ozymandias sarcastically suggested creating a 15-16 year old idol in skimpy clothes.

Some users, such as Dyaush and Yui, reminisced about past experiences and expressed fond memories of interacting with individuals from specific cultural backgrounds.

Dyaush mentioned 'NEpyari supremacy' and fondly recalled a 'momo wali pyaari /nep/yaari didi', while Yui shared a similar anecdote and bemoaned not being able to 'marry her'.

The discussion continued with users like Kantabai Ownes and Soyjeet weighing in on the potential group's composition and aesthetics. Kantabai Ownes expressed hope for an all-Indian group with variety, while Soyjeet described one potential member as resembling a 'rakshashi' from scriptures.

The thread featured various reactions, from Haggu's dismissive comments about certain groups to Anon's critique of a potential member's appearance. The conversation remained fixated on the hypothetical idol group and its possible implications.

articles.title: Hinduism Characterized as Philosophy and Way of Life
articles.body:
Anon initiated a discussion stating that Hinduism 'isn't a religion, it's a philosophy and way of life, but I will never ever tell what is that philosophy and way of life.' Another anon responded by criticizing the initial post, expressing that people often mistakenly believe a lack of evidence disproves something, calling this 'book definition of illiteracy.' This respondent also suggested that some individuals engage in discussions to provoke more knowledgeable participants.

One participant later clarified their position on atheism, noting that an atheist does not assert the non-existence of God but rather remains unconvinced by theistic claims. A rejoinder described all theists as agnostics.

The conversation also touched on the nature of Hinduism, with one poster asserting that at its core, Hinduism revolves around 'Varna Vyavastha,' and another describing it as 'a collection of diverse philosophies that share belief in Vedas.' The discussion involved various insults and personal attacks, with participants directing derogatory language at each other.

The topic of defining Hinduism and understanding religious or philosophical labels continued to be a point of contention among the respondents.

articles.title: Taxes on Foreign Goods and Reading Habits
articles.body:
Anon expressed frustration about high taxes on foreign goods, citing video games and toys like Hot Wheels as examples. They contrasted this with the government's spending on foreign weapons.

Another anon responded by questioning the value of buying physical books in the modern age, suggesting that pirated copies should be used instead. This poster also mocked those who prefer physical books, implying they are old-fashioned.

A different anon brought up the idea that the issue might be related to literacy rates, stating that many people no longer read for leisure due to the prevalence of doomscrolling.

They attributed this to a lack of reading culture, particularly among younger generations.

One respondent directly addressed OP's point, stating that high taxes, such as 50% on foreign goods like Hot Wheels, make it difficult to afford nice things in India. They also noted that the government purchases foreign weapons for billions.

Other comments included suggestions for alternatives, such as downloading free PDFs or using library genesis (libgen) for books. Some anons also made personal attacks, calling others "retards" or "pajeets" for their views on reading and purchasing habits.

The discussion also touched on the preference for physical books over e-books, with some anons stating they intentionally avoid e-books or prefer holding physical objects. One poster even joked about using a musalman xerox dukaan for spiral binding.

Throughout the thread, anons used derogatory language and insults, reflecting a toxic and argumentative tone.

articles.title: North Indian Women Physical Appearance
articles.body:
Anon posted a comment saying North Indian women look like a certain individual. The post included an image.

Anon questioned if North Indian women are generally considered attractive, citing a previous comment from someone who allegedly said they do not find North Indian women's physical appearance attractive.

Anon noted that fair-skinned upper-caste women have good facial structure but are held back by their diet. Another Anon joked that North Indian women have a distinct 'curry glow'.

Anon accused the original poster of trolling or having a fetish. Anon also claimed the image looked AI-generated.

articles.title: Anon claims to reevaluate hate after meeting people from 'hated' groups
articles.body:
Anon posted about being an online user who spread hate against Muslims, Biharis, and Dalits, but claimed to have reevaluated their views after interacting with people from these groups in person.

They stated that these individuals were kinder than expected and deserved government support. Another anon responded by mocking the OP's supposed change of heart, calling it a form of "larping" that collapses in the face of reality.

They sarcastically noted that the internet's "master race" often fails to translate to real-life interactions.

The discussion drew various reactions, including claims about the influence of certain castes in Bihar and jokes about the OP's supposed weakness in their hate. Anon also made derogatory comments about Muslims and other groups.
